config.script:
system.perspective.print('Line 2')
	self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp").props.text = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblCount").props.text = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("ColumnContainer").getChild("TabContainer").getChild("TblContainer").getChild("Table").props.data = ''  # Clear the table data
	from datetime import datetime
	system.perspective.print('Line 6')
	
	AreaName = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("areadd").props.value
	Station = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("stationdd").props.value
	cycleTime = self.getSibling("cycleTimetext").props.text  # Ensure this is a number (int)
	IssueType = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("issuetypedd").props.value
	StartTime = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("frmDate").props.value  # This should be a datetime object
	EndTime = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("toDate").props.value  # This should be a datetime object
	downtime = self.parent.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("downtimedd").props.value
	system.perspective.print('Line 15')
	if cycleTime == '':
	    cycleTime = 2.8
	# Check if all required fields are filled
	if AreaName != "" and StartTime != "" and EndTime != "":
	    # Ensure FromDate and EndDate are datetime objects
	    if isinstance(StartTime, datetime):
	        # If FromDate is a datetime object, convert to string format (yyyy-MM-dd)
	        StartTime = StartTime.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')	        
	    if isinstance(EndTime, datetime):
	        # If EndDate is a datetime object, convert to string format (yyyy-MM-dd)
	        EndTime = EndTime.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
	
	    # Ensure cycleTime is a number (integer)
	    try:
	        cycleTime = int(cycleTime)
	    except ValueError:
	        cycleTime = 10  # Default to 10 if the value is not an integer
	    system.perspective.print("resutl")
	    system.perspective.print('Line 34')
	    
	    # Run the named query to get the downtime details and populate the table
	    result = system.db.runNamedQuery('Project_VECV/Report/DownTimeReport/GetDowntimeDetails',
	        {
	            'AreaName': AreaName,
	            'Station': Station,
	            'cycleTime': cycleTime,
	            'IssueType': IssueType,
	            'StartTime': StartTime,  
	            'EndTime': EndTime,
	            'downtime': downtime
	        }
	    )
	    system.perspective.print('Line 48')
	
	    # Run the named query to get the downtime details and populate the table
	    resultCount = system.db.runNamedQuery(
	        'Project_VECV/Report/DownTimeReport/CountDowntimes',
	        {
	            'AreaName': AreaName,
	            'Station': Station,
	            'cycleTime': cycleTime,
	            'IssueType': IssueType,
	            'StartTime': StartTime,
	            'EndTime': EndTime,
	            'downtime': downtime
	        }
	    )
	
	    # Check if data is returned and set it to the table
	    if result:
	        self.parent.parent.getChild("ColumnContainer").getChild("TabContainer").getChild("TblContainer").getChild("Table").props.data = result
	
	    # Now, run the SumDowntime query to get the sum of downtime
	    sum_result = system.db.runNamedQuery(
	        'Project_VECV/Report/DownTimeReport/SumDowntime', {
	            'AreaName': AreaName,
	            'Station': Station,
	            'cycleTime': cycleTime,
	            'IssueType': IssueType,
	            'StartTime': StartTime,  
	            'EndTime': EndTime,
	            'downtime': downtime
	        }
	    )
	    sum_downtime = sum_result[0]['sum_downtime']
	    system.perspective.print(sum_downtime)
	    system.perspective.print('Line 75')
	    if sum_downtime is not None:
	        minutes = int(sum_downtime)  # Convert decimal part into seconds 
	        seconds = int(round((sum_downtime - minutes) * 60))
	        hour = int(sum_downtime) / 60
	        
	        if hour > 0:
	            minutes = int(sum_downtime % 60)
	            self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp").props.text = str(hour) + ' Hour ' + str(minutes) + ' Minutes ' + str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	        else:
	            if minutes > 0:
	                self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp").props.text = str(minutes) + ' Minutes ' + str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	            else:
	                self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp").props.text = str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	        
	    if resultCount:
	        count_downtime = resultCount[0]['count_downtime']
	        self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblCount").props.text = count_downtime
	    system.perspective.print('Line 100')
	    
	    #---------------------------
	    #system.perspective.print("downtime for conveyor before query----------->")
	    system.perspective.print('Line 104')
	    	
	    sum_resultConv = system.db.runNamedQuery(
	        'Project_VECV/Report/DownTimeReport/SumDowntimeConveyor', {
	            'AreaName': AreaName,
	            'StartTime': StartTime,  
	            'EndTime': EndTime
	        }
	    )
	    system.perspective.print('Downtime------------>')
	    system.perspective.print(downtime)
	    system.perspective.print('Line 116')
	    system.perspective.print(sum_resultConv)
	    system.perspective.print(sum_resultConv[0]['sum_downtime'])
	    sum_downtimeConv = sum_resultConv[0]['sum_downtime']
	    system.perspective.print('downtime for conveyor----------->')
	    system.perspective.print(sum_downtimeConv)
	    if sum_downtimeConv is not None:
	        minutes = int(sum_downtimeConv)  # Convert decimal part into seconds 
	        seconds = int(round((sum_downtimeConv - minutes) * 60))
	        hour = int(sum_downtimeConv) / 60
	        
	        if hour > 0:
	        	#system.perspective.print("Line 128 hour>0")
	            minutes = int(sum_downtimeConv % 60)
	            self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp_0").props.text = str(hour) + ' Hour ' + str(minutes) + ' Minutes ' + str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	        else:
	            if minutes > 0:
	            	system.perspective.print("Line 132 minute>0")
	                self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp_0").props.text = str(minutes) + ' Minutes ' + str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	            else:
	            	system.perspective.print("Line 132 minute<0")
	                self.parent.parent.getChild("OpContainer").getChild("lblSumDowntimeOp_0").props.text = str(int(seconds)) + ' Seconds'
	    else:
	    	system.perspective.print("-----")
		    	
		

			
	    #--------------------------------
	else:
	    system.perspective.print("Select Area and Date")

# Get the table data
	   table_data = self.parent.parent.getChild("ColumnContainer").getChild("TabContainer").getChild("TblContainer").getChild("Table").props.data
	   
	   # Check if the table contains data
	   if table_data is not None and len(table_data) > 0:
	       # If data is available, export it to Excel and trigger download
	       excel_data = system.dataset.toExcel(True, [table_data])
	       system.perspective.download(fileName="Downtime_Report.xlsx", data=excel_data)

print("Inside runAction")
	from java.util import Calendar
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 7:30 AM
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 7)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 30)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	# Set the value of the DateTime Picker component
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("frmDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 4:30 PM
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 16)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 30)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("toDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()

print("Inside runAction")
	from java.util import Calendar
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 4:30 pm
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 16)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 30)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	# Set the value of the DateTime Picker component
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("frmDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 11:59 PM
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 23)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 59)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("toDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()

print("Inside runAction")
	from java.util import Calendar
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 12:00 AM
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 00)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 00)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	# Set the value of the DateTime Picker component
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("frmDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()
	
	# Get today's date and set the time to 7:30 AM
	cal = Calendar.getInstance()
	cal.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 7)
	cal.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 30)
	cal.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
	cal.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
	print(cal)
	self.parent.getChild("InputContainer").getChild("toDate").props.value= cal.getTimeInMillis()
